NAME: Maximum amount of the collateral/guarantee that can be considered
DESCRIPTION: For banks this piece of information is a result of the allocation of collateral and guarantees on the instruments according to FinREP criteria. It is the maximum amount of the collateral or guarantee that can be considered as credit protection for the instruments. The sum of the amounts of the financial guarantee and/or collateral for a specific instrument shall not exceed the carrying amount or the nominal amount of the instrument. For instruments that have simultaneously more than one type of collateral or guarantee, the amount of the Maximum amount of the collateral/guarantee that can be considered shall be allocated according to its quality starting from the one with the best quality. For instruments collateralised by immovable property, immovable property collateral shall always be reported first, irrespective of its quality compared to other collateral. Where the Maximum amount of the collateral/guarantee that can be considered exceeds the value of immovable property collateral, its remaining value shall be allocated to other collateral types and guarantees according to its quality starting from the one with best quality

NAME: Probability of default
DESCRIPTION: The counterparty’s probability of default over one year determined in accordance with Articles 160, 163, 179 and 180 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

NAME: Null explanatory values
DESCRIPTION: Indicates the reasons for null values of variables (for instance, whether a null value in the staging area corresponds to a real null (i.e. the reporting agent has not reported any value) or to an error (i.e. the reporting agent reported

NAME: Exposure with recourse
DESCRIPTION: Classification of instruments based on the creditor’s rights to seize assets other than any protection pledged to secure the instrument.

NAME: Purpose
DESCRIPTION: Classification of instruments according to their purpose.

NAME: Arrears for the instrument
DESCRIPTION: Aggregate amount of principal, interest and any fee payment outstanding at the reporting date, which is contractually due and has not been paid (past due). This amount is always to be reported. '0' is to be reported if the instrument was not past due on the reporting date.

NAME: Fiduciary instrument
DESCRIPTION: Identification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.

NAME: Date of past due
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the instrument became past due in accordance with Part 2.48 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. This is the latest such date prior to the reporting reference date and it is to be reported if the instrument is past due on the reporting reference date.

NAME: Entity role
DESCRIPTION: Role of the counterparties in an instrument.

NAME: Reference date
DESCRIPTION: Reporting agents shall report credit data as recorded on the following reporting reference dates: (a) for monthly transmissions, on the last day of each month; (b) for quarterly transmissions, on the last day of March, June, September and December.

NAME: Reference rate
DESCRIPTION: Reference rate used for the calculation of the actual interest rate.

NAME: Inception date
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement became binding for all parties.

NAME: Protection identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each protection used to secure the instrument. Each protection must have one protection identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the protection identifier for any other protection.

NAME: Protection value
DESCRIPTION: The amount of the protection value as established for the relevant ‘Type of protection value’ following the valuation approach.

NAME: Default status
DESCRIPTION: Identification of the default status of the instrument or entity. Categories describing the situations in which an instrument or entity can be described as being at default in accordance with Article 178 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

NAME: Contract identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each contract. Each contract must have one contract identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the contract identifier for any other contract.

NAME: Settlement date
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the conditions specified in the contract are or can be executed for the first time, i.e. the date on which financial instruments are initially exchanged or created.

NAME: Type of protection
DESCRIPTION: Type of protection received, irrespective of its eligibility for credit risk mitigation.

NAME: Country where the collateral is located.
DESCRIPTION: Country where the collateral is located.

NAME: Instrument identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

NAME: Carrying amount
DESCRIPTION: The carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

NAME: Type of amortisation
DESCRIPTION: Type of amortisation of the instrument including principal and interest

NAME: Recognition
DESCRIPTION: Balance sheet recognition of the financial asset.

NAME: Subordinated debt
DESCRIPTION: Identification of subordinated debt. Subordinated debt instruments provide a subsidiary claim on the issuing institution that can only be exercised after all claims with a higher status (e.g. deposits/loans) have been satisfied.

NAME: Type of securitisation
DESCRIPTION: Identification of the securitisation type, in accordance with Article 242(10) and (11) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

NAME: Forbearance and renegotiation status
DESCRIPTION: Identification of forborne and renegotiated instruments.

NAME: Type of instrument
DESCRIPTION: Classification of the instrument according to the type of contractual terms agreed between the parties.

NAME: Performing status
DESCRIPTION: Performing status in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

NAME: Impairment status
DESCRIPTION: Type of impairment recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP.

NAME: Source of encumbrance
DESCRIPTION: Type of transaction in which the exposure is encumbered in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. An asset will be treated as encumbered if it has been pledged or if it is subject to any form of arrangement to secure, collateralise or credit enhance any instrument from which it cannot be freely withdrawn.

NAME: Payment frequency
DESCRIPTION: Frequency of payments due, either of principal or interest, i.e. number of months between payments.

NAME: Date of default status
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the default status is considered to have arisen.

NAME: Interest rate cap
DESCRIPTION: Maximum value for the interest rate charged.

NAME: Prudential portfolio
DESCRIPTION: Classification of exposures in the trading book as defined in Article 4(1)(86) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

NAME: Date of protection value
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to the reporting reference date.

NAME: Postal code where the collateral is located.
DESCRIPTION: Postal code where the collateral is located.

NAME: Repayment rights
DESCRIPTION: Classification of credit exposures according to the creditor's rights to claim the repayment of the exposure. On demand or at short notice

NAME: Accrued interest (accounting)
DESCRIPTION: The amount of accrued interest on loans at the reporting reference date as defined in Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33). In accordance with the general principle of accruals accounting, interest receivable on instruments should be subject to on-balance sheet recording as it accrues (i.e. on an accruals basis) rather than when it is actually received (i.e. on a cash basis).

NAME: Currency denomination of instruments
DESCRIPTION: Currency denomination of instruments, in accordance with the ISO’s 4217 standard

NAME: Type of protection value
DESCRIPTION: Identification of the type of the protection value.

NAME: Type of interest rate
DESCRIPTION: Classification of credit exposures based on the base rate for establishing the interest rate for each payment period.

NAME: Interest rate floor
DESCRIPTION: Minimum value for the interest rate charged.

NAME: Entity RIAD code
DESCRIPTION: The RIAD code is an alphanumeric code assigned to each entity recorded in RIAD. The RIAD code is exclusive, meaning that the same RIAD code cannot be allocated to more than one entity (even at different points in time), and is unique, meaning that a given entity has only one RIAD code. It is the responsibility of each NCB, for respective (resident) entities, to ensure that all entities with an official RIAD code represent distinct real world entities (unique identification).

NAME: Transferred amount
DESCRIPTION: Transferred amount of the economic ownership of the financial asset.

NAME: Project finance loan
DESCRIPTION: Identification of project finance to identify in case of a project finance loan in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

NAME: Interest rate
DESCRIPTION: Annualised agreed rate or narrowly defined interest rate in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1072/2013 of the European Central Bank (ECB/2013/34).

NAME: Interest rate spread / margin
DESCRIPTION: Margin or spread (expressed as a percentage) to add to the reference rate that is used for the calculation of the interest rate in basis points.

NAME: Observed agent identifier (identifier type: RIAD internal identifier)
DESCRIPTION: RIAD code of the observed agent

NAME: Commitment amount at inception
DESCRIPTION: Observed agent's maximum exposure to credit risk on the inception date of the instrument, without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements. Total commitment amount on the inception date is established during the approval process and is intended to restrict an observed agent's amount of credit risk to a given counterparty for the relevant instrument.

NAME: Accumulated write-offs
DESCRIPTION:
Cumulative amount of principal and past due interest of any debt instrument that the institution is no longer recognising because they are considered uncollectible, independently of the portfolio in which they were included. Write-offs could be caused both by reductions in the carrying amount of financial assets recognised directly in profit or loss and by reductions in the amounts of the allowance accounts for credit losses set off
against the carrying amount of financial assets.

NAME: Joint liability amount
DESCRIPTION: Outstanding nominal amount for which each debtor is liable in relation to a single instrument where there are two or more debtors.

NAME: Date of forbearance and renegotiation status
DESCRIPTION: The date on which a forbearance or renegotiation status as reported under 'status of forbearance and renegotiation' is considered to have occurred.

NAME: Accumulated impairment
DESCRIPTION:
The amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the instrument on the reporting reference date. This data attribute applies to instruments subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.

NAME: Original protection value
DESCRIPTION: The protection’s fair value at the date when it was originally received as a credit protection.

NAME: Date of the performing status of the instrument
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the performing status is considered to have been established or changed.

NAME: Protection provider identifier (identifier type: RIAD code)
DESCRIPTION: RIAD code of the protection provider. If the protection provider is not a legal entity, the protection provider identifier is not required to be reported.

NAME: Maturity date of the protection
DESCRIPTION: The contractual maturity date of the protection, which is the earliest date at which the protection may terminate or be terminated, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.

NAME: Region where the collateral is located.
DESCRIPTION: Region where the collateral is located.

NAME: Legal final maturity date
DESCRIPTION: The contractual maturity date of the instrument, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.

NAME: Protection allocated value
DESCRIPTION: The maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

NAME: Accounting classification
DESCRIPTION: Accounting portfolio where the instrument is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.

NAME: Syndicated contract identifier
DESCRIPTION: 'Contract identifier' applied by the lead arranger of the syndicated contract to uniquely identify each contract. Each syndicated contract will have one 'syndicated contract identifier'. This value will not change over time and cannot be used by the lead arranger as the contract identifier for any other contract. All creditors participating in the syndicated contract must use the same 'syndicated contract identifier'.

NAME: Off-balance sheet amount
DESCRIPTION: Total nominal amount of off-balance sheet exposures. This includes any commitment to lend before considering conversion factors and credit risk mitigation techniques. It is the amount that best represents the institution's maximum exposure to credit risk without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements.

NAME: Date of original protection value
DESCRIPTION: The date of the protection’s original value, i.e. the date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to its initial receipt as a credit protection.

NAME: End date of the interest-only period
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the interest-only period ends. Interest-only is an instrument for which, for a contractually set period, only the interest on the principal balance is paid, with the principal balance remaining unchanged.

NAME: Outstanding nominal amount
DESCRIPTION: Principal amount outstanding at the end of the reporting reference date, including unpaid past due interest but excluding accrued interest. The outstanding nominal amount must be reported net of write-offs and write-downs as determined by the relevant accounting practices.

NAME: Protection valuation approach
DESCRIPTION: Type of protection valuation; method used to determine the protection value.

NAME: Real estate collateral location
DESCRIPTION: Region or country where the collateral is located.

NAME: Provisions associated with off-balance sheet exposures
DESCRIPTION: The amount of provisions for off-balance sheet amounts.

NAME: Accumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk
DESCRIPTION: Accumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

NAME: Third party priority claims against the protection
DESCRIPTION: The maximum amount of any existing higher ranked liens with respect to third parties other than the observed agent against the protection.

NAME: Next interest rate reset date
DESCRIPTION: The date that the next interest rate reset, as defined in Part 3 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33), takes place. If the instrument is not subject to a future interest rate reset, its legal final maturity date will be reported.

NAME: Cumulative recoveries since default
DESCRIPTION: The total amount recovered since the date of default.

NAME: Interest rate reset frequency
DESCRIPTION: Frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any.

NAME: Fair value changes due to changes in credit risk before purchase
DESCRIPTION: The difference between the outstanding nominal amount and the purchase price of the instrument at the purchase date. This amount should be reported for instruments purchased for an amount lower than the outstanding amount due to credit risk deterioration.

NAME: Impairment assessment method
DESCRIPTION: The method by which the impairment is assessed, if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with applied accounting standards. Collective and individual methods are distinguished.

NAME: decimals
DESCRIPTION: The decimals facet indicates the number of characters allowed after the decimal separator.

NAME: precision
DESCRIPTION: The precision facet indicates the total number of digits in a number.

NAME: endTime
DESCRIPTION: The endTime facet is used in conjunction with the isSequence and timeInterval facets (which must be set in order to use this facet). This facet is used for a time sequence, and indicates that ending point (if any) of the sequence.

NAME: interval
DESCRIPTION: The interval attribute specifies the permitted interval (increment) in a sequence. In order for this to be used, the isSequence attribute must have a value of true.

NAME: endValue
DESCRIPTION: The endValue facet is used in conjunction with the isSequence and interval facets (which must be set in order to use this facet). This facet is used for a numeric sequence, and indicates that ending point (if any) of the sequence.

NAME: isSequence
DESCRIPTION: The isSequence facet indicates whether the values are intended to be ordered, and it may work in combination with the interval, startValue, and endValue facet or the timeInterval, startTime, and endTime, facets. If this attribute holds a value of true, a start value or time and a numeric or time interval must supplied. If an end value is not given, then the sequence continues indefinitely.

NAME: startTime
DESCRIPTION: The startTime facet is used in conjunction with the isSequence and timeInterval facets (which must be set in order to use this facet). This attribute is used for a time sequence, and indicates the start time of the sequence. This value is mandatory for a time sequence to be expressed.

NAME: startValue
DESCRIPTION: The startValue facet is used in conjunction with the isSequence and interval facets (which must be set in order to use this facet). This facet is used for a numeric sequence, and indicates the starting point of the sequence. This value is mandatory for a numeric sequence to be expressed.

NAME: timeInterval
DESCRIPTION: The timeInterval facet indicates the permitted duration in a time sequence. In order for this to be used, the isSequence facet must have a value of true.

NAME: Main category of hedging instrument
DESCRIPTION: Defines the main category of the instrument used for hedging.

NAME: Main category of hedged item
DESCRIPTION: Defines the main category of the item that is hedged.

NAME: Carrying amount
DESCRIPTION: The amount to be reported in the balance sheet. The carrying amount of financial instruments shall include accrued interest. (Annex V, part 1)

NAME: Notional amount
DESCRIPTION: The ‘Notional amount’ shall be the gross nominal of all deals concluded and not yet settled at the reference date, regardless of whether these deals lead to derivative exposures being booked on-balance sheet. (Annex V, Part 2, include further details)

NAME: Metric
DESCRIPTION:
Details the data type (e.g. monetary, percentage, string) of the data point. In XBRL, it is the "primary item". 
Generally, it indicates the "metrics" of the Main category provided (i.e. answers the question: how is measured?).
It should be defined for each data point. 
In the case of monetary values, when the data point is referred to a (change during a) period of time, the member name shall finish in "(flow)". When the member name has other terminations, the data point is a measurement at a date (i.e. "stock").

NAME: Accounting portfolio
DESCRIPTION: Accounting portfolios shall mean financial instruments aggregated by valuation rules (e.g. "Available-for-sale financial assets"). Accounting portfolios only comprise financial assets and financial liabilities.

NAME: Country of the market
DESCRIPTION: Defined the country in which is located the market on which the instrument is traded.

NAME: Main category that generates the provision
DESCRIPTION: Defines the main category that generates the related provisions.

NAME: Base
DESCRIPTION:
Defines the basic conceptual meaning of a data point. 
Identifies the framework in which a data point is included. For FINREP, it indicates in which group of element of the financial statement should be included the data point (e.g. assets, liabilities, equity, income, expenses). For COREP, it indicates whether the data point should be computed in the numerator (own funds) or the denominator (exposures) of the Pillar I solvency ratio. 
It determines whether the data point has a "debit" or a "credit" attribute.

NAME: Main category of the underlying
DESCRIPTION: Defines the type of instrument underlying another balance or transaction (e.g. short position).

NAME: Gross carrying amount
DESCRIPTION: Gross carrying amount of debt instuments as in Annex V, part 1 (different for different portfolios). In general carrying amount before adjusting for credit risk (allowances or negative fair value changes due to credit risk).

NAME: Instrument
DESCRIPTION: Defines instrument or product type of the main category item

NAME: Performing status
DESCRIPTION: Identifies the performing status of financial assets and off-balance sheet items (performing, non-performing...)

NAME: Type of risk
DESCRIPTION: Indicates the type of risk arising from exposures or transactions (e.g. credit risk or market risk).

NAME: Counterparty sector
DESCRIPTION: Defines the sector of the counterparty of financial instruments (e.g. Central banks or Credit institutions).

NAME: Own funds
DESCRIPTION: Specifies the eligibility for the various levels of own funds for the fully phased-in period (without transitional provisions).

NAME: Type of hedge
DESCRIPTION: Defines the type of hedging

NAME: Accumulated impairment
DESCRIPTION: Cumulative amount of impairment related to financial assets. Details for each portfolio in Annex V, Part 2.

NAME: Financial reporting standard
DESCRIPTION: Financial reporting standard for recognition, measurement and presentation (IFRS or national GAAP) to define the scope of reported items.

NAME: Purpose
DESCRIPTION: Defines the purpose of a balance or transaction.

NAME: Business line
DESCRIPTION: Defines the business line that generates operational risk.

NAME: Main category
DESCRIPTION:
Specifies the nature of the item reported (i.e. answers the question: what is reported?).
For financial instruments, defines the instrument (e.g. deposits, debt securities issued...) or range of instruments reported. 
It should be defined for each data point.

NAME: Derivatives Purchased/Sold
DESCRIPTION: For derivatives, it indicates whether the reporting institution has sold or purchased them.

NAME: Residence of counterparty
DESCRIPTION: Defines the geographical area where the counterparty of the contract or transaction resides.

NAME: Reference date or period
DESCRIPTION: Specifies the date or the period to which the data is referred

NAME: Size of the counterparty
DESCRIPTION: Specifies the size category of the counterpart

NAME: Unit
DESCRIPTION: Unit

NAME: Impairment status
DESCRIPTION: Status for monitoring credit quality of financial assets and off-balance sheet items (e.g. past due, impaired, defaulted).

NAME: Observation_Value
DESCRIPTION: Observation_Value

NAME: Accumulated negative changes in fair value due to credit risk
DESCRIPTION: For non-performing exposures, accumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk where the accumulated net change is negative. (Annex V, Part 2, include further details)

NAME: Approach for prudential purposes
DESCRIPTION: Approach used for the calculation of capital requirements.

NAME: Controlling and non-controlling owners
DESCRIPTION: For equity components, it indicates whether they belong to the owners of the parent or to non-controlling owners (minority interests).

NAME: Hedged or hedging instrument
DESCRIPTION: Defines hedging or hedged instrument of the main category item

NAME: Scope of consolidation
DESCRIPTION: Specifies the scope of consolidation of reporting when different from CRR scope

NAME: Type of activity
DESCRIPTION: Defines the type of activity (e.g. asset management or custody).

NAME: Main category of collateral or guarantee given
DESCRIPTION: Defines the main category of collateral or guarantees given

NAME: Location of the activities
DESCRIPTION: Identifies the nature of the geographical area where activities are undertaken. "Location" means the jurisdiction of incorporation of the legal entity which has recognised the asset or liability; for branches, it means the jurisdiction of residence.

NAME: Type of risk transfer
DESCRIPTION: Defines the way in which a risk has been transferred in a securitisation (traditional or synthetic securitisations).

NAME: Related parties/Relationships
DESCRIPTION: Defines the relationship between the reporting institution with an entity or a person; in particular, it specifies the type of related party.

NAME: Type of market
DESCRIPTION: Defines the type of market on which reported instruments are traded.

NAME: Prudential portfolio
DESCRIPTION: Defines whether it is reported the trading book business, the "banking" book business, or both of them.

NAME: Forbearance status
DESCRIPTION: Identifies the financial assets and off-balance sheet items with forbearance measures and, if applicable, the type of forbearance measures

NAME: General interest rate risk (GIRR) delta Risk position (Kb)
DESCRIPTION:
delta related  KB as defined in bcbs d457 21.4 (4)

Within bucket aggregation: the risk position for delta (respectively vega) bucket b, must be determined by aggregating the weighted sensitivities to risk factors within the same bucket using the prescribed correlation set out in the following formula, where the quantity within the square root function is floored at zero

https://www.bis.org/bcbs/publ/d457.pdf

NAME: Market capitalisation indicator
DESCRIPTION:
Market capitalisation indicator classifies the Listed central bank and private sectory company as Large or Small market capitalisation. 

Market capitalisation, or "market cap", of the Listed central bank and private sector company is the sum of the market capitalisations based on the market value of the total outstanding shares issued by the same listed legal entity or a group of legal entities across all stock markets globally, where the total outstanding shares issued by the group of legal entities refer to cases where the listed entity is a parent company of a group of legal entities.
Large market cap is defined as a market capitalisation equal to or greater than USD 2 billion and small market cap is defined as a market capitalisation of less than USD 2 billion.
(https://www.bis.org/basel_framework/chapter/MAR/21.htm?inforce=20230101&published=20200327)

DESCRIPTION: Type of business entity as defined in the national legal system.

NAME: Name
DESCRIPTION: Full legal name of the entity, e.g. as in the business register.

NAME: Entry date
DESCRIPTION: The date in which the investee entered within the ‘scope of the group’ Annex V 296

NAME: Fair value hierarchy
DESCRIPTION: Level of fair value of a financial instruments according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.

NAME: Notional amount
DESCRIPTION: The notional amount on a financial instrument is the nominal or face amount that is used to calculate payments made on that instrument.

NAME: Voting rights (%)
DESCRIPTION: Percentages of voting rights associated to the ownership instruments held by the institution as of the reference date.

NAME: Nominal amount
DESCRIPTION: Holding amount at nominal value

NAME: Enterprise size
DESCRIPTION: Classification of enterprises by size, in accordance with the Annex to Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC.

NAME: Annual turnover
DESCRIPTION: Annual sales volume net of all discounts and sales taxes in accordance with Recommendation 2003/361/EC. Equivalent to the concept of 'total annual sales' in Article 153(4) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

NAME: Fair value option designation
DESCRIPTION: Defines the reason to irrevocably designate a non derivative financial instrument at fair value through profit or loss (use of fair value option). Is not applicable for nGAAP based on BAD.

NAME: Acquisition cost
DESCRIPTION: The amount paid by the investors  FinRep Annex V 296.

NAME: Litigation status
DESCRIPTION:
Litigation indicates the status of the litigation that the exposure is in.

Finrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.

NAME: Territorial unit
DESCRIPTION: Entity's territorial unit according to Regulation (EC) No 1059/2003 on the establishment of a common classification of territorial units for statistics (NUTS).

NAME: Number of employees
DESCRIPTION: Number of employees working for the counterparty, in accordance with Article 5 of the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.

NAME: Party identifier type
DESCRIPTION: Party identifier type classifies the Other party code according to its nature.

NAME: High liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) indicator
DESCRIPTION: An indicator specifying if an asset or collateral received is considered extremely high liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) eligible according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex 17 (Asset encumbrance), part 2, 2.1.2, column 035.

NAME: Group of clients identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each group.

NAME: Current loan-to-value ratio
DESCRIPTION: Current loan-to-value ratio (LTV-C) means the sum of all loans or loan tranches secured by the borrower on a property at the reporting date relative to the current value of the property (ESRB), allocated per Loan (excluding repurchase agreement) derived data.

NAME: Other party code
DESCRIPTION: Other party code is an identifier that identifies the party externally to the bank.

NAME: Economic activity
DESCRIPTION: Classification of counterparties according to their economic activities, in accordance with the NACE revision 2 statistical classification as laid down in Regulation (EC) No 1893/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council.

NAME: Balance sheet total
DESCRIPTION: Carrying value of total assets in accordance with Regulation (EU) 549/2013.

NAME: Date of enterprise size
DESCRIPTION: The date to which the value provided in the ‘enterprise size’ refers. This is the date of the latest data used to classify or review the classification of the enterprise.

NAME: Internal group identifier
DESCRIPTION: Internal group identifier is an identifier to uniquely identify each internal group involved in the Internal group Key management personnel assignment.

NAME: Held for sale indicator
DESCRIPTION: A variable indicating the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.

NAME: Forbearance measure
DESCRIPTION: A discriminator to distinguish between refinanced debt, instruments with modified interest rate below market value and other modified terms.

NAME: Derived default status
DESCRIPTION: Derived default status is the default status where all specific statusses of default for the Financial asset instrument have been mapped to either "Default" or "Not in default".

NAME: Initial recognition value
DESCRIPTION:
Initial recognition value is an amount, expressed in EUR, that is the value of the Collateral obtained by taking possession at the moment of recognising it in the balance sheet.

FINREP Annex V:
Value at initial recognition shall mean the gross carrying amount of the collateral obtained by taking possession at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet of the reporting institution.

NAME: Collateral obtained by taking possession identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.

NAME: Software collateral identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.

NAME: Accumulated coverage ratio
DESCRIPTION: It is calculated as the ratio between the accumulated impairments, respectively the accumulated negative changes in fair value due to credit risk related to a loan or advance as numerator, and the gross carrying amount of that loan or advance as denominator.

NAME: Loan and advance identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

NAME: Institutional unit identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each group.

NAME: SCRTSN_RFRNC_DT
DESCRIPTION: SCRTSN_RFRNC_DT

NAME: Legal proceeding status
DESCRIPTION: Categories describing an entity's legal status in relation to its/his/her solvency based on the national legal framework.

NAME: Accumulated changes in fair value
DESCRIPTION: Amount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.

NAME: The reason for the choice of Performing status
DESCRIPTION: The reason for the choice of Performing status

NAME: Equity instrument identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

NAME: Obtained collateral type
DESCRIPTION: Obtained collateral type distinguishes Collateral whether it is a collateral obtained by taking possession and other collaterals that are not obtained.

NAME: Short name of the instrument
DESCRIPTION: Short name given by issuer, to be defined according to the characteristics of the issue and the available information.

NAME: Gross carrying amount
DESCRIPTION: Gross carrying amount as defined in Annex V 5.2.

NAME: Allocated collateral value uncapped pre haircuts
DESCRIPTION: Allocated collateral value uncapped pre haircuts is an amount that is the value of the collateral that is not used for risk mitigations (uncapped) and before any haircuts have been applied, and that is for FINREP reporting "allocated" as unused amount to the Loan (excluding repurchase agreemtn) and advance.

NAME: Physical collateral identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.

NAME: Natural person Party identifier
DESCRIPTION: Natural person is an identifier that uniquely identifies the natural person involved in the Natural person Key management personnel assignement.

NAME: Covered bond program identifier
DESCRIPTION: The covered bond program identifier allows the identification of a Covered bond program. Such a Covered bond program may include an asset portfolio and (one-or-many) covered bonds issued.

NAME: Off-balance instrument reference date
DESCRIPTION: The date the data refers to.

NAME: EXPSR_RCRS_INDCTR
DESCRIPTION: Exposure with recourse indicator specifies if a Financial asset instrument is an instrument with or without recourse.

NAME: Accumulated negative changes
DESCRIPTION: Accumulated negative changes shall be the difference, at the level of the individual collateral item, between the value at initial recognition of the collateral and the carrying amount at the reporting reference date, where that difference is negative. (FINREP Annex V)

NAME: TM_SNC_INTL_RCGNTN
DESCRIPTION: Time elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.

NAME: Accumulated total write-offs
DESCRIPTION: According to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated (...) total amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."

NAME: Accumulated equity interest
DESCRIPTION: Accumulated equity interest (%)’ shall be the percentage of ownership instruments held by the institution as of the reference date FinRep Annex V 296

NAME: Group of clients reference date
DESCRIPTION: The date the data refers to.

NAME: Hedged items fair value changes
DESCRIPTION: Fair value changes of the hedged items in portfolio hedge of interest rate risk

NAME: Loan and advance leg identifier
DESCRIPTION: The instrument identifier of the involved repurchase agreement.

NAME: Software assets indicator
DESCRIPTION: Software asset indicator indicates whether the reported asset is a software asset or not.

NAME: Domestic activities indicator
DESCRIPTION: Domestic activities indicator distinguishes the Interest rate risk hedge portfolio between domestic and non-domestic activities from the perspective of the reporting agent.

NAME: Low credit risk indicator for Finrep
DESCRIPTION: Low credit risk indicator for Finrep

NAME: Internal group Reference date
DESCRIPTION: Internal group Reference date is the date the data of the Internal group Key management personnel assignment refers to.

NAME: Transfer between impairment stages
DESCRIPTION: Transfer between impairment stages compares the impairment status at the beginning of the period (Initial impairment status) with the impairment status at the end of the period.

NAME: Equity instrument leg identifier
DESCRIPTION: The instrument identifier of the involved repurchase agreement.

NAME: Accumulated partial write-offs
DESCRIPTION: According to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated partial (...) amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."

NAME: International organisation code
DESCRIPTION: Pseudo-ISO code for international organisations of the Eurostat’s ‘Balance of Payments Vademecum’.

NAME: Planning permission for development indicator
DESCRIPTION: Planning permission for development indicator distinguishes Land excluding agriculture based on whether planning permission for development applies or not.

NAME: Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value
DESCRIPTION:
Haircut for trading position valued at fair value for the Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP). 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.

NAME: Off-balance instrument identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

NAME: Subject to impairment indicator
DESCRIPTION: Subject to impairment indicator indicates whether the asset is subject to impairment or not.

NAME: Natural person group role type

NAME: Transferred asset leg type
DESCRIPTION: Transferred asset leg type distinguishes Transferred asset leg according to the type of asset that is transferred.

NAME: Exchange tradable derivative identifier
DESCRIPTION: An identifier to uniquely identify each security.

NAME: Date of initiation of legal proceedings
DESCRIPTION: The date on which the legal proceedings, as reported under the attribute ‘status of legal proceedings’, were initiated. This date should be the most recent relevant date prior to the reporting date and should only be reported if the data attribute 'Status of legal proceedings' has a value other than 'No legal actions taken'.

NAME: Under construction or development indicator
DESCRIPTION:
Under construction or development indicator indicates whether the property is under construction or development.

Finrep Annex V. Part 2.350, 352(a). With regard to collateral in the form of immovable property (a) immovable property that is under construction or development.

It can be used to report the amount derecognised for capital purposes in FINREP.
